texts.orig: Facilitated transport of CO2 through polyethylenimine/poly(vinyl alcohol) blend membrane
texts.text: Facilitated transport of CO2 through polyethylenimine/poly(vinyl alcohol) blend membrane

texts.label: text

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Abstract
texts.text: Abstract

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Polyethylenimine (PEI)/poly(vinyl alcohol) (PVA) blend membranes were prepared for the facilitated transport of CO2. The polymeric carrier PEI was retained in the blend membrane by the entanglement with PVA chains. The CO2 permeance decreased with an increase in CO2 partial pressure in feed gas, whereas the N2 permeance was nearly constant. This result clearly showed that only CO2 was transported by the facilitated transport mechanism. The CO2 and N2 permeabilities increased monotonically with the PEI weight percent in the blend membrane, whereas the selectivity of CO2 over N2 showed a maximum. The selectivity increased remarkably with increasing heat-treatment temperature of the membrane. The highest selectivity obtained reached more than 230 when the CO2 partial pressure was 0.065 atm. The prepared membrane was stable. ©1999 Elsevier Science B.V. All rights reserved.
texts.text: Polyethylenimine (PEI)/poly(vinyl alcohol) (PVA) blend membranes were prepared for the facilitated transport of CO2. The polymeric carrier PEI was retained in the blend membrane by the entanglement with PVA chains. The CO2 permeance decreased with an increase in CO2 partial pressure in feed gas, whereas the N2 permeance was nearly constant. This result clearly showed that only CO2 was transported by the facilitated transport mechanism. The CO2 and N2 permeabilities increased monotonically with the PEI weight percent in the blend membrane, whereas the selectivity of CO2 over N2 showed a maximum. The selectivity increased remarkably with increasing heat-treatment temperature of the membrane. The highest selectivity obtained reached more than 230 when the CO2 partial pressure was 0.065 atm. The prepared membrane was stable. ©1999 Elsevier Science B.V. All rights reserved.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. Introduction
texts.text: 1. Introduction

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Separation and recovery of CO2 has attracted attention from the viewpoint of preventing global warming. Membrane separation is one of the more promising and realistic methods of low energy consumption. Haraya et al. investigated the application of membrane separation to the CO2 removal from flue gases [1]. They indicated that a selectivity of CO2 over N2 of more than 210 is required to recover CO2 efficiently by a single stage permeator. However, a selectivity of more than 100 has been hardly reported for polymeric membranes.
texts.text: Separation and recovery of CO2 has attracted attention from the viewpoint of preventing global warming. Membrane separation is one of the more promising and realistic methods of low energy consumption. Haraya et al. investigated the application of membrane separation to the CO2 removal from flue gases [1]. They indicated that a selectivity of CO2 over N2 of more than 210 is required to recover CO2 efficiently by a single stage permeator. However, a selectivity of more than 100 has been hardly reported for polymeric membranes.

texts.text: Corresponding author.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: On the other hand, facilitated transport membranes show fairly high selectivity as well as high permeability owing to the carrier incorporated in the membrane [2]. Facilitated transport membranes for gas separation at the early stage of their development were prepared by impregnating the pores of microporous supports with carrier solutions [3-6]. This type membrane is known as a supported liquid membrane (SLM) or a immobilized liquid membrane. Although this type of membrane showed remarkably high selectivity, membrane degradation due to the evaporation of the membrane solution and/or the 'washout' of the carrier was recognized as the serious problem.
texts.text: On the other hand, facilitated transport membranes show fairly high selectivity as well as high permeability owing to the carrier incorporated in the membrane [2]. Facilitated transport membranes for gas separation at the early stage of their development were prepared by impregnating the pores of microporous supports with carrier solutions [3-6]. This type membrane is known as a supported liquid membrane (SLM) or a immobilized liquid membrane. Although this type of membrane showed remarkably high selectivity, membrane degradation due to the evaporation of the membrane solution and/or the 'washout' of the carrier was recognized as the serious problem.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: brane degradation. When an ionic carrier is immobilized in an ion exchange membrane by the attractive electrostatic force, the washout of carrier is prevented. LeBlanc et al. first reported the facilitated transport of CO2 and C2H4 through an ion exchange membrane immobilizing ionic carriers [7]. Since then, a number of papers have been published on the facilitated transport of gases through this type of membrane [8-16].
texts.text: brane degradation. When an ionic carrier is immobilized in an ion exchange membrane by the attractive electrostatic force, the washout of carrier is prevented. LeBlanc et al. first reported the facilitated transport of CO2 and C2H4 through an ion exchange membrane immobilizing ionic carriers [7]. Since then, a number of papers have been published on the facilitated transport of gases through this type of membrane [8-16].

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In this facilitated transport membrane, the morphology of the ion exchange membrane such as its ion exchange capacity and the distribution of ionic sites greatly affects the membrane performance. We prepared a cation exchange membrane by grafting acrylic acid onto a microporous polyethylene membrane by a plasma-grafted polymerization technique and studied the facilitated transport of CO2 through the grafted membrane containing ethylenediamine as a carrier [17,18]. The obtained membrane showed remarkably high selectivity of CO2 over N2. For example, when the CO2 partial pressure in the feed gas was 0.047 atm, the selectivity reached 4700. This efficiency was much higher than that of polymer membranes and also that of conventional ion exchange membrane containing carriers. This results was attributable to the high ion exchange capacity and the high water content of the prepared ion exchange membrane.
texts.text: In this facilitated transport membrane, the morphology of the ion exchange membrane such as its ion exchange capacity and the distribution of ionic sites greatly affects the membrane performance. We prepared a cation exchange membrane by grafting acrylic acid onto a microporous polyethylene membrane by a plasma-grafted polymerization technique and studied the facilitated transport of CO2 through the grafted membrane containing ethylenediamine as a carrier [17,18]. The obtained membrane showed remarkably high selectivity of CO2 over N2. For example, when the CO2 partial pressure in the feed gas was 0.047 atm, the selectivity reached 4700. This efficiency was much higher than that of polymer membranes and also that of conventional ion exchange membrane containing carriers. This results was attributable to the high ion exchange capacity and the high water content of the prepared ion exchange membrane.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In these facilitated transport membranes, the carrier must be introduced into the ion exchange membrane by impregnating them with carrier solutions, which may make the membrane preparation method troublesome. Further, although the attractive electrostatic force between carrier and ion exchange membrane can certainly contribute to the prevention of the carrier leakage to some extent, this prevention is not perfect since the carrier is lost especially in the presence of other ions.
texts.text: In these facilitated transport membranes, the carrier must be introduced into the ion exchange membrane by impregnating them with carrier solutions, which may make the membrane preparation method troublesome. Further, although the attractive electrostatic force between carrier and ion exchange membrane can certainly contribute to the prevention of the carrier leakage to some extent, this prevention is not perfect since the carrier is lost especially in the presence of other ions.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In this work, a facilitated transport membrane composed of a polymeric carrier of polyethylenimine (PEI) and poly(vinyl alcohol) (PVA) was prepared. PEI can act as a carrier of CO2 because it contains primary and secondary amino groups which can react with CO2 to form carbamate. It is expected that the entanglement of the polymeric carrier with PV A chains and the small pore size of PV A lead to the retainment of carrier in the membrane. Further, since PEI and PVA are highly hydrophilic, the aqueous membrane solution can be efficiently retained in the membrane, which brings about higher membrane stability. A PVA membrane with aminated weak base has been already prepared [19], although the membrane types are different. However, as far as we know, the facilitated transport of gases through such a membrane has not been reported so far.
texts.text: In this work, a facilitated transport membrane composed of a polymeric carrier of polyethylenimine (PEI) and poly(vinyl alcohol) (PVA) was prepared. PEI can act as a carrier of CO2 because it contains primary and secondary amino groups which can react with CO2 to form carbamate. It is expected that the entanglement of the polymeric carrier with PV A chains and the small pore size of PV A lead to the retainment of carrier in the membrane. Further, since PEI and PVA are highly hydrophilic, the aqueous membrane solution can be efficiently retained in the membrane, which brings about higher membrane stability. A PVA membrane with aminated weak base has been already prepared [19], although the membrane types are different. However, as far as we know, the facilitated transport of gases through such a membrane has not been reported so far.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Experimental materials and methods
texts.text: 2. Experimental materials and methods

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2.1. Materials
texts.text: 2.1. Materials

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Two kinds of PEI with different molecular weight were used. PEIs with the weight average molecular weight of 750 000 and 2000 were purchased from Aldrich as 50 wt.% aqueous solutions. The ratio of primary amino group : secondary amino group : tertiary amino group in two PEIs are approximately equal to 1 : 2 : 1. Hereafter, PEIs with molecular weight (MW of 750000 and 2000 are abbreviated as PEI (1) and PEI (2), respectively. PVA (Aldrich, 99 + % hydrolyzed powder) has an average molecular weight of 89 000-98 000.
texts.text: Two kinds of PEI with different molecular weight were used. PEIs with the weight average molecular weight of 750 000 and 2000 were purchased from Aldrich as 50 wt.% aqueous solutions. The ratio of primary amino group : secondary amino group : tertiary amino group in two PEIs are approximately equal to 1 : 2 : 1. Hereafter, PEIs with molecular weight (MW of 750000 and 2000 are abbreviated as PEI (1) and PEI (2), respectively. PVA (Aldrich, 99 + % hydrolyzed powder) has an average molecular weight of 89 000-98 000.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2.2. Preparation of PEI/PVA blend membrane
texts.text: 2.2. Preparation of PEI/PVA blend membrane

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The PEI/PVA blend membrane was prepared as follows. The dope solutions were prepared by adding a 20 wt.% PEI solution to a 10 wt.% PVA solution. The composition of the solution was varied by adjusting the weight ratio of the two solutions. The dope solutions was cast onto a glass plate with the thickness of 1000 m m and placed in a drying oven at 60 C for 10 h. The blend membrane was finally obtained by heat-treating the dried membrane at several temperatures (usually 140 C) for 1 h. The glass plate with the membrane was immersed in water bath to detach the membrane.
texts.text: The PEI/PVA blend membrane was prepared as follows. The dope solutions were prepared by adding a 20 wt.% PEI solution to a 10 wt.% PVA solution. The composition of the solution was varied by adjusting the weight ratio of the two solutions. The dope solutions was cast onto a glass plate with the thickness of 1000 m m and placed in a drying oven at 60 C for 10 h. The blend membrane was finally obtained by heat-treating the dried membrane at several temperatures (usually 140 C) for 1 h. The glass plate with the membrane was immersed in water bath to detach the membrane.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2.4. Permeation experiments
texts.text: 2.4. Permeation experiments

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The apparatus used in the gas permeation experiments and procedure were similar to those described in our previous work [17,18]. The permeation cell consisted of two components (depth: 3.7 mm) for a feed and a sweep gas. The membrane was sandwiched between the two components. The feed gas, a mixture of CO2 and N2 (CO2 flow rate: 12.3-105 cm 3 /min, N2 flow rate: 200 cm 3 /min) and the sweep gas, helium (flow rate: 69.4 cm 3 /min) were supplied to the cell at atmospheric pressure. Both gas stream were saturated with water upstream of the cells. The fluxes of CO2 and N2 were calculated from the sweep gas flow rate and the outlet sweep gas composition, which was analyzed by a gas chromatograph equipped with a thermal conductivity detector (Shimadzu, GC-8APT). The measurements of the permeation rates were carried out in a constant temperature environment maintained at 25 C.
texts.text: The apparatus used in the gas permeation experiments and procedure were similar to those described in our previous work [17,18]. The permeation cell consisted of two components (depth: 3.7 mm) for a feed and a sweep gas. The membrane was sandwiched between the two components. The feed gas, a mixture of CO2 and N2 (CO2 flow rate: 12.3-105 cm 3 /min, N2 flow rate: 200 cm 3 /min) and the sweep gas, helium (flow rate: 69.4 cm 3 /min) were supplied to the cell at atmospheric pressure. Both gas stream were saturated with water upstream of the cells. The fluxes of CO2 and N2 were calculated from the sweep gas flow rate and the outlet sweep gas composition, which was analyzed by a gas chromatograph equipped with a thermal conductivity detector (Shimadzu, GC-8APT). The measurements of the permeation rates were carried out in a constant temperature environment maintained at 25 C.

PEI with Mw of 2000 showed low retention factor. Thus, most of the PEI of low molecular weight leaks from the membrane.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Table 2 shows the retention factors of PEI (1) in the blend membranes heat-treated at several temperatures. With increasing temperature, the retention factor increased remarkably. Chu and Yang investigated the effect of heat-treatment of PVA membrane on water permeation through the membrane [21]. They showed that as the heat-treatment time increased, the density of crosslinking which was formed by the condensation reaction between molecular chains increased, which resulted in the lower water permeation rate. Similarly to the increase of the treatment time, the increase of the heat-treatment temperature is considered to enhance the crosslinking density. The increase of the retention factor at the higher temperature in Table 2 is attributable to the decrease in the pore size of PVA

texts.orig: The membrane thickness and water content of the blend membranes with several PEI wt.% are shown in Fig. 1. As the PEI wt.% increased, the membrane thickness and water content increased monotonically. This is because the membrane became more swollen in the condition of the higher content of PEI, which is more hydrophilic than PVA. The water content reached about 0.8 in the blend membrane with the PEI wt.% of 47.4%.

This is because the membrane became more swollen in the condition of the higher content of PEI, which is more hydrophilic than PVA. The water content reached about 0.8 in the blend membrane with the PEI wt.% of 47.4%.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The result of the PEI/PVA blend membrane is shown in Fig. 4. In contrast to the results shown in Fig. 3, the CO2 permeance and the selectivity decreased with the pressure, whereas the N2 permeance was nearly constant. If CO2 transport follows the facilitated transport mechanism, CO2 flux increases proportionally with the CO2 partial pressure in the low pressure region and reaches a constant value at a pressure where the carrier is saturated on the feed side [8]. Therefore, when the data are plotted as the relation between the permeance and the partial pressure, the permeance decreases at the pressure where the carrier becomes saturated. This is characteristic of the facilitated transport mechanism and this result clearly demonstrates the PEI functions efficiently as the carrier of CO2 in the blend membrane.
texts.text: The result of the PEI/PVA blend membrane is shown in Fig. 4. In contrast to the results shown in Fig. 3, the CO2 permeance and the selectivity decreased with the pressure, whereas the N2 permeance was nearly constant. If CO2 transport follows the facilitated transport mechanism, CO2 flux increases proportionally with the CO2 partial pressure in the low pressure region and reaches a constant value at a pressure where the carrier is saturated on the feed side [8]. Therefore, when the data are plotted as the relation between the permeance and the partial pressure, the permeance decreases at the pressure where the carrier becomes saturated. This is characteristic of the facilitated transport mechanism and this result clearly demonstrates the PEI functions efficiently as the carrier of CO2 in the blend membrane.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: PEI wt.%. The initial increase of the selectivity is due to the increase in the amount of carrier in the membrane. However, when PEI wt.% became higher, the retention factor of PEI in the membrane decreased as shown in Table 1, and furthermore, the membrane became more swollen, which leads to a decrease in the PEI concentration in the membrane [22]. Due to these two factors, the selectivity decreases. On the other hand, the permeabilities of both CO2 and N2 increased monotonically with the PEI wt.%. As the membrane swells, the diffusivity in the membrane usually becomes larger. Thus, the higher swelling of the membrane at the higher PEI wt.% leads to the higher permeability. When the PEI wt.% is 32.7%, the CO2 permeability was 8.5 10 -8 (cm 3 cm/(cm 2 s cmHg)) with a selectivity of more than 160. Since the membrane thickness in this case was 220 m m, the permeance is 3.9 10 -6 (cm 3 /(cm 2 s cmHg)). This value is fairly low compared to that of our previous membrane (1.0 10 -4 (cm 3 /(cm 2 s cmHg))) [17,18]. However, if a membrane with a thickness of 10 m m can be prepared and the permeability is the same as that of the thick membrane, a permeance of 8.5 10 -5 (cm 3 /(cm 2 s cmHg)) can be obtained. This value is comparable to that of the previous membrane.
texts.text: PEI wt.%. The initial increase of the selectivity is due to the increase in the amount of carrier in the membrane. However, when PEI wt.% became higher, the retention factor of PEI in the membrane decreased as shown in Table 1, and furthermore, the membrane became more swollen, which leads to a decrease in the PEI concentration in the membrane [22]. Due to these two factors, the selectivity decreases. On the other hand, the permeabilities of both CO2 and N2 increased monotonically with the PEI wt.%. As the membrane swells, the diffusivity in the membrane usually becomes larger. Thus, the higher swelling of the membrane at the higher PEI wt.% leads to the higher permeability. When the PEI wt.% is 32.7%, the CO2 permeability was 8.5 10 -8 (cm 3 cm/(cm 2 s cmHg)) with a selectivity of more than 160. Since the membrane thickness in this case was 220 m m, the permeance is 3.9 10 -6 (cm 3 /(cm 2 s cmHg)). This value is fairly low compared to that of our previous membrane (1.0 10 -4 (cm 3 /(cm 2 s cmHg))) [17,18]. However, if a membrane with a thickness of 10 m m can be prepared and the permeability is the same as that of the thick membrane, a permeance of 8.5 10 -5 (cm 3 /(cm 2 s cmHg)) can be obtained. This value is comparable to that of the previous membrane.

texts.orig: The effect of the heat-treatment temperature is shown in Fig. 7. The PEI (1) was used in this experiment. The N2 permeability decreased with increase in temperature, whereas the CO2 permeability was nearly independent of the temperature, which leads to the increase of the selectivity with temperature.
texts.text: The effect of the heat-treatment temperature is shown in Fig. 7. The PEI (1) was used in this experiment. The N2 permeability decreased with increase in temperature, whereas the CO2 permeability was nearly independent of the temperature, which leads to the increase of the selectivity with temperature.

texts.text: Fig. 8. Stability test of the blend membrane. Mw of PEI: 750 000, PEI wt.%: 24.8%, CO2 partial pressure: 0.065 atm, heat treatment: 140 C for 1 h.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The highest selectivity was more than 230. As the heat-treatment temperature increases, the water content decreases, as can be seen in Fig. 2. The less swollen membrane structure leads to a lower diffusivity in the membrane, which causes the decrease of the N2 permeability. With the increase in the temperature, the retention factor of PEI increased, as shown in Table 2. Further, the lower water content in the membrane heat-treated at the higher temperature brings about the higher PEI concentration. For these reasons, the CO2 permeability did not decrease with the temperature in spite of the decrease of the diffusivity.
texts.text: The highest selectivity was more than 230. As the heat-treatment temperature increases, the water content decreases, as can be seen in Fig. 2. The less swollen membrane structure leads to a lower diffusivity in the membrane, which causes the decrease of the N2 permeability. With the increase in the temperature, the retention factor of PEI increased, as shown in Table 2. Further, the lower water content in the membrane heat-treated at the higher temperature brings about the higher PEI concentration. For these reasons, the CO2 permeability did not decrease with the temperature in spite of the decrease of the diffusivity.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Fig. 8 shows the stability test of the blend membrane. The blend membrane showed roughly constant selectivity for more than 7 days, although the CO2 and N2 permeances decreased slightly. This stability is probably attributable to both the high hydrophilicity of the blend membrane and the retainment of PEI by entanglement with PVA chains.
texts.text: Fig. 8 shows the stability test of the blend membrane. The blend membrane showed roughly constant selectivity for more than 7 days, although the CO2 and N2 permeances decreased slightly. This stability is probably attributable to both the high hydrophilicity of the blend membrane and the retainment of PEI by entanglement with PVA chains.

texts.orig: It is important to know whether the polymeric carrier PEI is fixed or mobile in the membrane. It is speculated that polymers with a Mw of 750 000 entangled in the crosslinked matrix with low pore size can not carry all solutes through the membrane. Therefore, the polymeric carrier is almost fixed in the membrane and CO2 may jump from one to the other amino group suc-

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 4. Conclusions
texts.text: 4. Conclusions

texts.label: text
texts.orig: PEI/PVA blend membranes were prepared and the facilitated transport of CO2 was performed. The CO2 permeance decreased with the increase in the CO2 partial pressure, whereas the N2 permeance was nearly constant. This result showed that only CO2 was transported by the facilitated transport mechanism and also that PEI functioned efficiently as the carrier of CO2.
texts.text: PEI/PVA blend membranes were prepared and the facilitated transport of CO2 was performed. The CO2 permeance decreased with the increase in the CO2 partial pressure, whereas the N2 permeance was nearly constant. This result showed that only CO2 was transported by the facilitated transport mechanism and also that PEI functioned efficiently as the carrier of CO2.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The effect of the PEI wt.% in the blend membrane on the permeability and the selectivity was investigated. The CO2 and N2 permeabilities increased monotonically with increasing PEI wt.%. This is because the membrane became more swollen at the condition of the higher PEI wt.%. On the other hand, the selectivity showed a maximum. The highest selectivity was about 160 and was higher than that of the PVA membrane. As the heat-treatment temperature increased, the selectivity increased remarkably. The selectivity reached more than 230 by heat-treatment at 160 C.
texts.text: The effect of the PEI wt.% in the blend membrane on the permeability and the selectivity was investigated. The CO2 and N2 permeabilities increased monotonically with increasing PEI wt.%. This is because the membrane became more swollen at the condition of the higher PEI wt.%. On the other hand, the selectivity showed a maximum. The highest selectivity was about 160 and was higher than that of the PVA membrane. As the heat-treatment temperature increased, the selectivity increased remarkably. The selectivity reached more than 230 by heat-treatment at 160 C.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The blend membrane was stable for 7 days. Both the high hydrophilicity of the blend membrane and the retainment of PEI by entanglement with PVA chains probably brought about this stability.
texts.text: The blend membrane was stable for 7 days. Both the high hydrophilicity of the blend membrane and the retainment of PEI by entanglement with PVA chains probably brought about this stability.
